summary: Provision brand-new Amazon Neptune graph-database clusters and their cluster instances. No Neptune estate exists yet, so every operation is a first stand-up an engineer reviews before it applies.

operations.title: Provision a new Neptune cluster
operations.summary: Request a brand-new Neptune graph-database cluster; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a graph database as a complete specification: network placement, backups and encryption. This provisions the cluster only; at least one instance is added afterward with the companion cluster-instance form. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish.

params.name: cluster_name
params.label: Cluster identifier
params.help: Lower-case name AWS uses for the cluster endpoint.

params.name: engine
params.label: Engine
params.help: Neptune has one engine value — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: engine_version
params.label: Engine version
params.help: Leave blank for the engineer to pin the version.

params.name: backup_retention_days
params.label: Keep backups (days)
params.help: Days of automatic backups. This baseline never allows zero, which would mean no backups.

params.name: preferred_backup_window
params.label: Backup window (UTC)
params.help: Leave blank for AWS to choose a quiet slot.

params.name: storage_encrypted
params.label: Storage encryption
params.help: Always on from birth — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: kms_key
params.label: Encryption key
params.help: Leave blank to use the default AWS-managed key. Pick a key only for a customer-managed CMK.

params.name: subnet_group
params.label: Network placement
params.help: Which private subnets the cluster lives in. Empty until the estate provisions or imports a Neptune subnet group.

params.name: security_groups
params.label: Security groups
params.help: Who can reach the cluster's port.

params.name: deletion_protection
params.label: Deletion protection
params.help: Always on from birth — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Cluster name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this cluster holds
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this cluster.

decisions: Cluster and instance parameter group choice and tuning
IAM database authentication and the neptune IAM roles list are engineer decisions
At least one aws_neptune_cluster_instance is required before the cluster serves traffic — use the companion cluster-instance form after this is approved
Global cluster membership is an engineer decision

operations.title: Provision a new Neptune cluster instance
operations.summary: Add a new instance to an existing Neptune cluster; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new cluster instance as a complete specification: which cluster it joins and instance class. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: cluster
params.label: Cluster
params.help: The Neptune cluster this instance joins. Create the cluster first if the list is empty.

params.name: identifier
params.label: Instance identifier
params.help: Lower-case name AWS uses for this instance's own endpoint.

params.name: engine
params.label: Engine
params.help: Neptune has one engine value — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: instance_class
params.label: Instance class
params.help: Size class for processor and memory.

params.name: promotion_tier
params.label: Failover promotion tier
params.help: Lower tiers are promoted to primary first during failover. 0 is highest priority.

params.name: publicly_accessible
params.label: Public accessibility
params.help: Never public. These instances are internal-only — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Instance name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this instance serves
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this instance.

decisions: Custom parameter group is an engineer decision
Additional read replicas: submit this form again once per extra instance
